summary: I was born in Laos, where my life and family were deeply affected by the conflicts that took place there. My husband joined the fight in Laos during the Secret War, and we lived through many hardships together. Unfortunately, the war brought great loss to my family while we were still in Laos before I eventually came to the United States in 1978.

My husband joined the Secret War in Laos, starting by transporting weapons in the village of Laj Huab. Later, when the Americans arrived, he enlisted for combat around 1960. This war brought tragedy to my family, as my husband, children, and grandson were all killed by the communists during the conflict.

I arrived in the United States in 1978 after fleeing the violence in Laos. The United States became my new home, but I carry the memories of my lost family members who were killed during the war back in Laos. It was a place of refuge after so much suffering and loss.

Laj Huab was the village where my husband first helped by transferring weapons during the early days of the Secret War. It was a significant place in the beginning of his involvement in the conflict. This village marks the start of the dangerous path that eventually led to the loss of my husband and family.

The Communist Party of Laos was the force that pursued us and caused the deaths of my husband, children, and grandson. They followed us as we fled and were responsible for killing my loved ones, including my husband who died in my arms. Their actions brought immense grief and loss to my family during the war.
